laws.title: Public Law 9-62 | Repealing the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals the District of Columbia Home Rule Act of 2010 and restores or revives the law amended or repealed by it.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repeal was identified in the reviewed materials, and the source itself does not self-repeal or sunset.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act is a repeal-only District of Columbia measure; no surviving transfer or redesignation requires operative Code text.
laws.status_basis: The source is an operative local/District repeal act that does not create a new positive-law section and is best preserved as a concise history note.

current_implementation.detail: The current note sits in the D.C. charter subject area, but it still carries a source dump and should be reduced to a concise repeal-history entry.

recommended_actions: Trim the note to a concise repeal-history statement.
Remove the quoted source dump and keep the D.C. home-rule repeal as history only.

laws.title: Public Law 9-63 | The Federal District Employment Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Source unavailable; no operative statutory text can be verified.
laws.chronology: Source-limited. The recovered materials do not provide readable statutory text, so no lawful chronology beyond note-only treatment can be fixed from the source itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain could be resolved from the available source.
laws.status_basis: The source text is unrecoverable, so the law remains a historical note without inventing operative text.

current_implementation.detail: A default Title 5 placement was chosen despite no recoverable statutory text. The entry should stay source-limited only.

recommended_actions: Retain only a concise source-limited historical note.
Do not invent operative text or a new code section.

laws.title: Public Law 10-65 | Federal Judicial Reform Act ii
laws.final_statutory_text: Court opinion and order, not enacted public-law text; it declares challenged sections unconstitutional and null and void.
laws.chronology: The supplied source is judicial output rather than legislation, so no lawful positive-law codification can be derived from it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No transfer or redesignation issue is present because the source is not enactment text.
laws.status_basis: The source is a court opinion, not a public-law enactment, and it invalidates the challenged provisions.

current_implementation.detail: A judicial opinion was treated like a statutory note and parked in an unrelated title. The source should remain historical only.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Keep only a short historical cross-reference note, if any.

laws.title: Public Law 10-66 | The Americans First Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals the Diplomatic Relations Act of 1978 and Public Law 3-17, with immediate effect.
laws.chronology: Current repeal enactment; the source does not self-repeal or expire.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act operates as a repeal cross-reference for prior diplomatic-relations measures; no new permanent Code section is required.
laws.status_basis: The source is an operative repealer with immediate effect, but it does not create a new positive-law section for codification.

current_implementation.detail: The note is dump-heavy and anchored in an awkward subject area; it should be reduced to a concise repeal cross-reference.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Keep only concise historical notes recording the repeals.

laws.title: Public Law 10-67 | Ambassadorial Readjustment Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates ambassador nomination and acting rules, multi-portfolio assignment rules, portfolio geography rules, five ambassadorial portfolios, and related notice requirements in Title 22.
laws.chronology: Current general foreign-affairs legislation; the source contains no self-repeal or sunset.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 3 is a blanket conflict clause, but the substantive operative text belongs in a new Title 22 provision rather than a repeal-only note.
laws.status_basis: The act creates permanent ambassadorial rules and needs real Title 22 codification, not a note alone.

current_implementation.detail: The current treatment is only a boilerplate note with a dump and Trello link; the substantive foreign-affairs rules still need real codification.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Codify the substantive Section 2 rules as a new Title 22 provision near 22 U.S.C. 2651a.

laws.title: Public Law 10-68 | American Jobs Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Public Law 3-20, the National Immigration Employment Act of 2021, and takes immediate effect upon passage.
laws.chronology: Current repeal enactment; it does not self-repeal.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act is a historical repeal cross-reference for Public Law 3-20; no new Code section is required.
laws.status_basis: The law is an operative repealer with immediate effect, so a concise historical note is sufficient.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is parked in the wrong subject area and still carries a full-text dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Record the repeal of Public Law 3-20 as a concise historical cross-reference.

laws.title: Public Law 11-69 | DC Criminal Code Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a local District of Columbia criminal code and traffic offense regime; no federal U.S. Code insertion is appropriate.
laws.chronology: Current local/District enactment; the source does not self-repeal or expire.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act is local/District criminal-code material and should not be converted into permanent federal Code text.
laws.status_basis: The source creates enforceable D.C. criminal and traffic rules, but it belongs outside permanent federal U.S. Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The current note treats a local D.C. criminal code as if it were federal permanent text. It should remain local history only.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Exclude this local/District criminal code from permanent U.S. Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 11-70 | Supreme Court Please Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Transfers the Marshal of the United States Supreme Court and Supreme Court Police duties to the United States Marshals Service and terminates Supreme Court Police employees.
laws.chronology: Current transfer statute; the source does not self-repeal or expire.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act is a live transfer of duties tied to 40 U.S.C. 6121 and should be moved to that anchor rather than kept as a bare note.
laws.status_basis: The source directs a substantive transfer under Title 40, so a mere note is not enough.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is anchored in the wrong title and only captures a boilerplate summary; the real target is 40 U.S.C. 6121.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Move the transfer to the 40 U.S.C. 6121 anchor and codify the duty transfer there.

laws.title: Public Law 11-71 | Supreme Court Needs Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and replaces it with new Supreme Court membership and quorum text.
laws.chronology: Current direct-amendment enactment; the source contains no self-repeal or sunset.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 2(a)-(b) is a direct strike-and-replace amendment to 28 U.S.C. 1; the act requires actual textual codification.
laws.status_basis: The act directly rewrites 28 U.S.C. section 1, so the existing note treatment is insufficient.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked at the right anchor but remains boilerplate with a dump and Trello link; the actual section text still needs replacement.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Actually amend 28 U.S.C. 1 to the new Chief Justice plus four associate justices text.

laws.title: Public Law 11-72 | No More Mariah Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Public Law 10-65 and replaces 28 U.S.C. section 133 with a new District of Columbia judgeship rule.
laws.chronology: Current direct-amendment enactment; the source itself does not self-repeal.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 2 is a repeal cross-reference for Public Law 10-65, while section 3 requires a real amendment to 28 U.S.C. 133.
laws.status_basis: The act directly replaces Title 28 text and also records a repeal, so the draft note is not sufficient by itself.

current_implementation.detail: The note is anchored at section 1, but the real target is 28 U.S.C. 133 and the repeal of Public Law 10-65 also needs to be captured.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Amend 28 U.S.C. 133 with the new District of Columbia judgeship text.
Record the repeal of Public Law 10-65 as repeal history.
